steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the RELATIVE sizes of the two capitals' populations — i.e., directly compare Hanoi's population with Ulaanbaatar's.

steps.title: Look for explicit comparison
steps.content: Choice C (‘Even though Hanoi (population 7,781,631) is larger than Ulaanbaatar (population 907,802), Ulaanbaatar accounts for more of its country's population.’) uses the comparative word "larger": "Hanoi (population 7,781,631) is larger than Ulaanbaatar (population 907,802)." That structure foregrounds the size difference rather than just listing numbers.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Mongolia's capital is Ulaanbaatar, which has 907,802 people, and Vietnam's capital is Hanoi, which has 7,781,631 people.’: lists the two populations without comparing them; reader must do the work. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Comparing Vietnam and Mongolia, 7,781,631 is 8.14 percent of Vietnam's population, and 907,802 is 31.98 percent of Mongolia's.’: compares each city's share of its country, which is the wrong dimension (not relative sizes of the capitals). ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Even though Hanoi (population 7,781,631) is larger than Ulaanbaatar (population 907,802), Ulaanbaatar accounts for more of its country's population.’: explicit comparative emphasizes the relative sizes. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘The populations of the capitals of Mongolia and Vietnam are 907,802 (Ulaanbaatar) and 7,781,631 (Hanoi), respectively.’: pairs cities with numbers but, like A, omits any comparative language. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
